requirements.evidence: LSToolbar pins the row to `theme.control.minHeight` at ios/LaneShadow/Views/Molecules/LSToolbar.swift:76. The task/design contract requires `sizing.component.toolbarHeight`, which is 56 in tokens/semantic/dimensions.tokens.json.
requirements.remediation: Expose and use the dedicated toolbar-height token for the rendered frame instead of `theme.control.minHeight`, then add a behavior test that asserts the resolved height is 56.

requirements.evidence: `safeAreaInset(edge: .top, spacing: 0)` is present at ios/LaneShadow/Views/Molecules/LSToolbar.swift:58, and the forbidden hardcoded status-bar-height literals are absent from the file.
Large-title mode renders `LSText(title, variant: .opinion.lg)` in its own row at ios/LaneShadow/Views/Molecules/LSNavHeader.swift:85-95.

Default mode renders `LSText(title, variant: .ui.title.md)` inline in the toolbar row at ios/LaneShadow/Views/Molecules/LSNavHeader.swift:103-106, and there is no `NavigationView`/`NavigationStack` in the molecule source.

Static grep over LSToolbar.swift and LSNavHeader.swift returned zero matches for `Color(red:)`, `Color(hex:)`, `Font.system`, and `foregroundColor(`. `swiftformat --lint` on the touched molecule/test files also passed.

All 4 toolbar and 3 nav-header story IDs are present in ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/Stories/Molecules/LSToolbarStory.swift and ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/Stories/Molecules/LSNavHeaderStory.swift, and both story groups are aggregated in ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/Stories/MoleculesStories.swift.

Host validation evidence reports `cd ios && xcodebuild build -scheme LaneShadow -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16'` passed. Local replay in this sandbox was blocked by CoreSimulator/temp-file I/O restrictions, not by a source-level build failure.

findings.evidence: Both molecules render their chrome rows with `theme.control.minHeight`, which is the generic 48pt control token. The task contract and design reference require the dedicated toolbar height token (`sizing.component.toolbarHeight` = 56). This is a user-visible size regression, and the toolbar implementation directly violates AC-1.
findings.fix: Add a runtime path for the 56pt toolbar-height token and use it for both toolbar rows. Do not leave the token as a string constant; the rendered frame must resolve from the actual token value.

findings.evidence: The added tests are mostly source-string inspections and constant checks (`heightTokenPath`, `surfaceTokenPath`, `source.contains(...)`) rather than behavior assertions against rendered views. They missed the real regression above: the code advertises `sizing.component.toolbarHeight` but actually renders at `theme.control.minHeight`.
findings.fix: Replace source-text assertions with behavior tests that inspect rendered layout/token outcomes, including actual toolbar/header height and typography usage. Keep one AC-focused test per acceptance criterion, but make each test fail on real visual/token regressions.

findings.evidence: The nav-header chrome uses compact spacing tokens (`theme.space.xs` and `theme.space.md`) where the design recipe specifies 16pt horizontal and bottom padding for the nav-header chrome. That means the large-title/header spacing does not match the documented molecule recipe even though the typography variant is correct.
findings.fix: Align nav-header chrome spacing with the design token recipe for this molecule, then add a layout-focused test or snapshot-style assertion that covers the large-title spacing contract.

summary: Needs fixes. The implementation ships a real chrome-metric regression: both molecules render at the generic 48pt control height instead of the required 56pt toolbar token, and the new tests are too superficial to catch it. Static lint/forbidden-API gates passed; local xcodebuild replay was sandbox-blocked, so build/test status beyond that relies on the supplied host evidence.
